files.description: The authoritative Deployment struct definition in the Kubernetes API types repository. Defines the Deployment type with TypeMeta, ObjectMeta, DeploymentSpec (replicas, selector, template, strategy), and DeploymentStatus fields. This is the canonical data model for all Kubernetes Deployment resources.

Canonical Go code example for creating, updating, listing, and deleting Deployments using the client-go library. Shows the deploymentsClient pattern: clientset.AppsV1().Deployments(namespace) then deploymentsClient.Create(deployment). Sets replicas and container spec in the DeploymentSpec.

Developer documentation explaining the full deployment workflow: Creating deployment, Updating deployment (scale replicas + rolling update), Listing deployments, Deleting deployment. Explains the two update strategies (replace vs patch) and provides kubectl commands for verification.

text: Canonical Kubernetes service deployment patterns across repos: 1) API type definition (sg-evals/kubernetes-api, apps/v1/types.go): The Deployment struct is defined here with all required fields including replicas, selector, and template. This is the authoritative source for the Deployment data model — all services use this type. 2) Client-side code pattern (sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go, examples/create-update-delete-deployment/main.go): The canonical Go pattern uses deploymentsClient := clientset.AppsV1().Deployments(namespace) to get a typed client, then deploymentsClient.Create(deployment) to create a Deployment resource. The example sets 2 replicas and shows the full CRUD lifecycle. 3) Workflow documentation (sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go, examples/create-update-delete-deployment/README.md): The README documents the 4-step workflow: Create 2-replica Deployment, Update replica count and container image, List all Deployments, Get and Delete the Deployment. Together, these three files establish the canonical deployment pattern: define your service using apps/v1 Deployment types, create it with the typed deploymentsClient, and follow the documented Create-Update-List-Delete workflow.
